key_insight: A persistent-variable compositional model can stably simulate coupled heat, multiphase flow, and salt precipitation in fractured geothermal reservoirs without manual phase switching.
engineering_relevance: It helps predict permeability damage, wellbore blockage, and energy-recovery loss caused by halite scaling in high-enthalpy geothermal operations.
method: Uses pressure, enthalpy, and overall salt mass fraction as primary variables.
Combines a discrete fracture-matrix formulation with correlation-based saltwater phase-behavior linearization.
Updates porosity and permeability via the Kozeny-Carman relation during halite precipitation.
evidence_signal: The model is verified against the CSMP++ simulator in a 1D salt-dissolution benchmark and then demonstrated on a 2D fractured reservoir case with injection and production.
watch_item: Accuracy and robustness may depend on the quality of the thermodynamic correlations used to replace explicit phase-separation calculations.
paper_title: Mathematical Modeling of Salt Precipitation and Multi-Phase Flow in High Enthalpy Fractured Geothermal Systems
